entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: A major warm-season cereal grown primarily for grain production in arid and semi-arid tropical regions of Asia and Africa

entities.name: IRON DENSITY
entities.description: The concentration of iron in the grain of pearl millet

entities.name: ZINC DENSITY
entities.description: The concentration of zinc in the grain of pearl millet

entities.name: ASIA
entities.description: A geographical region where pearl millet is primarily grown

entities.name: AFRICA
entities.description: A geographical region where pearl millet is primarily grown

entities.name: MICRONUTRIENT MALNUTRITION
entities.description: A primary health problem affecting nearly two billion people worldwide caused by deficiencies in micronutrients like iron and zinc

entities.name: BREEDING
entities.description: A cost-effective and sustainable process used to improve the micronutrient densities of staple crops

entities.name: GENETICS
entities.description: The study of heredity and variation of micronutrients in pearl millet

entities.name: HETEROSIS
entities.description: The phenomenon where hybrid offspring exhibit superior traits compared to their parents

entities.name: FULL DIALLEL CROSSES
entities.description: A breeding design involving crosses between all possible pairs of inbred lines to study gene action and heterosis

entities.name: GENE ACTION
entities.description: The mechanism by which genes influence the expression of traits such as iron and zinc density

entities.name: GENERAL COMBINING ABILITY
entities.description: A measure of the average performance of a parent in its progeny used to analyze gene action

entities.name: SPECIFIC COMBINING ABILITY
entities.description: A measure of the performance of a hybrid relative to the expected performance based on the general combining ability of its parents

entities.name: PREDICTABILITY RATIO
entities.description: A mathematical ratio used to determine the preponderance of additive gene action

entities.name: ADDITIVE GENE ACTION
entities.description: A type of gene action where the effect of alleles is cumulative, found to be predominant for iron and zinc densities in pearl millet
